name: getTMDBEpisodeCollection
comments: getTMDBEpisodeCollection builds an EpisodeCollection from local LibraryEpisode records.
Used for TMDB-only media that don't have Platform metadata.

name: HandleGetAnimeEpisodeCollection
comments: HandleGetAnimeEpisodeCollection ...
@summary gets list of main episodes
@desc This returns a list of main episodes for the given anime media id (Platform or TMDB).
@desc It also loads the episode list into the different modules.
@returns anime.EpisodeCollection
@param id - int - true - "Anime media ID (positive=Platform, negative=TMDB)"
@route /api/v1/anime/episode-collection/{id} [GET]

api.summary: gets list of main episodes
descriptions: This returns a list of main episodes for the given anime media id (Platform or TMDB).
It also loads the episode list into the different modules.

params.name: id
descriptions: Anime media ID (positive=Platform, negative=TMDB)

name: HandleGetLibraryCollection
comments: HandleGetLibraryCollection returns the main local anime collection.
@summary returns the main local anime anime collection.
@desc This creates a new LibraryCollection struct and returns it.
@desc This is used to get the main anime collection of the user.
@desc It uses the cached platform anime collection for the GET method.
@desc It refreshes the platform anime collection if the POST method is used.
@route /api/v1/library/collection [GET,POST]
@returns anime.LibraryCollection

api.summary: returns the main local anime anime collection.
descriptions: This creates a new LibraryCollection struct and returns it.
This is used to get the main anime collection of the user.
It uses the cached platform anime collection for the GET method.
It refreshes the platform anime collection if the POST method is used.

name: HandleGetAnimeCollectionSchedule
comments: HandleGetAnimeCollectionSchedule returns the anime collection schedule.
@summary returns anime collection schedule
@desc This is used by the "Schedule" page to display the anime schedule.
@route /api/v1/library/schedule [GET]
@returns []anime.ScheduleItem

api.summary: returns anime collection schedule
descriptions: This is used by the "Schedule" page to display the anime schedule.

name: HandleAddUnknownMedia
comments: HandleAddUnknownMedia adds the given media to the user's Platform planning collections.
@summary adds the given media to the user's Platform planning collections
@desc Since media not found in the user's Platform collection are not displayed in the library, this route is used to add them.
@desc The response is ignored in the frontend, the client should just refetch the entire library collection.
@route /api/v1/library/unknown-media [POST]
@returns Platform.AnimeCollection

api.summary: adds the given media to the user's Platform planning collections
descriptions: Since media not found in the user's Platform collection are not displayed in the library, this route is used to add them.
The response is ignored in the frontend, the client should just refetch the entire library collection.

name: enrichEpisodesWithTMDB
comments: enrichEpisodesWithTMDB fetches episode-level metadata from TMDB and fills in
any fields that AniDB/Animap left empty (title, overview/summary, still image, runtime).
Supports multi-season series by fetching all available seasons in parallel.
This is purely additive â€” never overwrites existing non-empty values.

name: enrichMediaWithTMDB
comments: enrichMediaWithTMDB fetches series-level metadata from TMDB if the local description is missing.

name: HandleGetAnimeEntry
comments: HandleGetAnimeEntry returns a media entry for the given anime media id.
@summary return a media entry for the given anime media id.
@desc This is used by the anime media entry pages to get all the data about the anime.
@desc This includes episodes and metadata (if any), list data, download info...
@route /api/v1/library/anime-entry/{id} [GET]
@param id - int - true - "Anime media ID"
@returns anime.Entry

api.summary: return a media entry for the given anime media id.
descriptions: This is used by the anime media entry pages to get all the data about the anime.
This includes episodes and metadata (if any), list data, download info...

params.name: id
descriptions: Anime media ID

name: HandleGetAnimeEntrySuggestions
comments: HandleGetAnimeEntrySuggestions returns anime suggestions for the given local files.
@summary returns anime suggestions for the given local files.
@desc Accepts either a directory path (dir) or explicit file paths (paths[]).
@desc The frontend codegen contract sends dir; the legacy backend contract sends paths.
@route /api/v1/library/anime-entry/suggestions [POST]
@param dir - string - false - "Directory of the local files (frontend contract)"
@param paths - []string - false - "Explicit file paths (legacy contract)"
@returns []*platform.UnifiedMedia

api.summary: returns anime suggestions for the given local files.
descriptions: Accepts either a directory path (dir) or explicit file paths (paths[]).
The frontend codegen contract sends dir; the legacy backend contract sends paths.

params.name: dir
descriptions: Directory of the local files (frontend contract)

params.name: paths
descriptions: Explicit file paths (legacy contract)

bodyFields.name: Dir
descriptions: Frontend codegen sends dir (a directory path)
Frontend codegen sends dir (a directory path)

name: HandleUpdateAnimeEntryProgress
comments: HandleUpdateAnimeEntryProgress updates the progress of an anime entry.

@param mediaID - int - true - "Anime media ID"
@param progress - int - true - "New progress"
@returns bool

api.summary: updates the progress of an anime entry.
descriptions: This is used when the user manually updates the progress of an anime.

params.name: mediaID
descriptions: Anime media ID

params.name: progress
descriptions: New progress

@param mediaID - int - true - "Anime media ID"
@param repeat - int - true - "New repeat count"
@returns bool

descriptions: This is used when the user manually updates the repeat count of an anime.

params.name: mediaID
descriptions: Anime media ID

params.name: repeat
descriptions: New repeat count

name: HandleAnimeEntryManualMatch
comments: HandleAnimeEntryManualMatch is used to manually match a group of local files to a media.

@desc It will create a new scanner and run it for the selected files.
@route /api/v1/library/anime-entry/manual-match [POST]
@returns bool

api.summary: manually match a group of local files to a media.
descriptions: It will create a new scanner and run it for the selected files.

name: HandleAnimeEntryUnmatch
comments: HandleAnimeEntryUnmatch will unmatch the given local files.
@summary unmatch the given local files.
@desc It will set the mediaID of the files to 0 and remove the metadata.
@route /api/v1/library/anime-entry/unmatch [POST]
@returns bool

api.summary: unmatch the given local files.
descriptions: It will set the mediaID of the files to 0 and remove the metadata.

name: HandleDeletePlatformEntry
comments: HandleDeletePlatformEntry will delete the given media entry from Platform.

comments: HandleToggleAnimeEntrySilenceStatus toggles the silence flag (no-op until DB column exists).

@route /api/v1/library/anime-entry/silence [POST]
@returns bool

name: HandleLogin
comments: HandleLogin logs in the user by saving the JWT token in the database.
@summary logs in the user by saving the JWT token in the database.
@desc This is called when the JWT token is obtained after logging in with redirection on the client.
@route /api/v1/auth/login [POST]
@returns handlers.Status

api.summary: logs in the user by saving the JWT token in the database.
descriptions: This is called when the JWT token is obtained after logging in with redirection on the client.

name: HandleLogout
comments: HandleLogout logs out the user by removing JWT token from the database.
@summary logs out the user by removing JWT token from the database.
@route /api/v1/auth/logout [POST]
@returns handlers.Status

api.summary: logs out the user by removing JWT token from the database.

name: HandleSamsungDiscover
comments: HandleSamsungDiscover scans the local network for Tizen Smart TVs using SSDP
and falls back to HTTP health checks for previously paired TVs.

name: HandleSamsungPing
comments: HandleSamsungPing checks if a specific Samsung TV is reachable via HTTP.

name: HandleSamsungPaired
comments: HandleSamsungPaired returns the list of previously paired Samsung TVs.

name: HandleSamsungLaunch
comments: HandleSamsungLaunch launches a URL on a TV (via DLNA if supported, otherwise falling back to Samsung WebSocket browser launch).

name: getSSDPHeader
comments: Helper to extract a header value from SSDP response

name: resolveDLNADevice
comments: resolveDLNADevice fetches and parses the device description XML to find friendly name and AVTransport control URL.

name: discoverSamsungTVs
comments: discoverSamsungTVs performs SSDP multicast discovery across all network interfaces
to find Samsung Smart TVs and generic DLNA Media Renderers on the local network.

name: scanInterfaceForTVs
comments: scanInterfaceForTVs sends SSDP M-SEARCH packets from a specific local IP
and collects DLNA Media Renderer / Samsung TV responses.

name: isCastableDeviceResponse
comments: isCastableDeviceResponse checks if an SSDP response belongs to a Samsung TV or DLNA Media Renderer.

name: enrichWithPairedTVs
comments: enrichWithPairedTVs checks previously paired TVs via HTTP and adds any
that are online but were missed by SSDP discovery.

name: loadPairedTVs
comments: loadPairedTVs reads all previously paired TVs from the token file.

name: fetchTVInfo
comments: fetchTVInfo queries the Samsung TV HTTP API for its name and MAC addresses.

name: isTVReachable
comments: isTVReachable performs a quick HTTP health check against the TV's API.

name: containsIP
comments: containsIP checks if a device list already contains a TV with the given IP.

name: FetchDLNADeviceInfo
comments: FetchDLNADeviceInfo retrieves and parses the device description XML from a LOCATION URL.

name: ResolveURL
comments: ResolveURL helper resolves relative URLs based on the base location URL.

name: BuildDIDLMetadata
comments: BuildDIDLMetadata generates escaped DIDL-Lite metadata for media player renderers.

name: SendDLNASOAPAction
comments: SendDLNASOAPAction sends a SOAP XML request to the DLNA renderer.

name: DLNASetAVTransportURI
comments: DLNASetAVTransportURI sends the media URL to the DLNA renderer.

name: DLNAPlay
comments: DLNAPlay sends the play command to the DLNA renderer.

name: DLNAStop
comments: DLNAStop sends the stop command to the DLNA renderer.

name: HandleCastPlayer
comments: HandleCastPlayer serves a minimal HTML5 video player page for Smart TVs.
The TV browser opens this page to play the stream directly.

name: HandleCastGo
comments: HandleCastGo redirects to the last cast URL for easy manual TV entry.
Users can bookmark this URL on their TV browser for instant playback.

name: buildPlayerHTML
comments: buildPlayerHTML returns the <video> tag and player script for the given stream URL.
HLS streams use HLS.js; direct files use native <video>.

name: sendWakeOnLAN
comments: sendWakeOnLAN sends a magic packet to wake a TV from standby.

name: connectToTV
comments: connectToTV establishes a WebSocket connection to the Samsung TV.
It tries WSS (port 8002) first, then falls back to WS (port 8001),
and finally attempts Wake-on-LAN if the TV is unreachable.

name: connectWithWoL
comments: connectWithWoL sends WoL packets and retries the WebSocket connection.

name: getMACsForTV
comments: getMACsForTV reads stored MAC addresses for a given TV IP.

name: waitForAuthorization
comments: waitForAuthorization reads WebSocket messages until the TV authorizes or rejects the connection.

name: fetchInstalledApps
comments: fetchInstalledApps requests the list of installed apps from the TV.

name: findBrowserAppID
comments: findBrowserAppID determines the correct browser app ID from installed apps.

name: sendLaunchCommands
comments: sendLaunchCommands tries multiple strategies to open the browser on the TV.

name: launchURLOnSamsungTV
comments: launchURLOnSamsungTV connects to the TV via WebSocket, authorizes,
detects the browser app, and launches the target URL.

name: HandleGetCollection
comments: HandleGetCollection returns the TMDB collection by ID.
It fetches from the TMDB platform and returns a CollectionResponse that matches
the MediaCollectionData frontend interface. Collection-level metadata (poster,
backdrop, overview) is sourced from: DB cache â†’ TMDB live â†’ entry-level fallback.

name: HandleListCollections
comments: HandleListCollections returns all persisted franchise collections.

name: HandleUpdateContinuityWatchHistoryItem
comments: HandleUpdateContinuityWatchHistoryItem updates the watch history item.
@summary Updates watch history item.
@desc This endpoint is used to update a watch history item.
@desc Since this is low priority, we ignore any errors.
@route /api/v1/continuity/item [PATCH]
@returns bool

api.summary: Updates watch history item.
descriptions: This endpoint is used to update a watch history item.
Since this is low priority, we ignore any errors.

name: HandleGetContinuityWatchHistoryItem
comments: HandleGetContinuityWatchHistoryItem returns a watch history item.

@desc This endpoint is used to retrieve a watch history item.
@route /api/v1/continuity/item/{id} [GET]
@param id - int - true - "Platform anime media ID"
@returns continuity.WatchHistoryItemResponse

api.summary: Returns a watch history item.
descriptions: This endpoint is used to retrieve a watch history item.

params.name: id
descriptions: Platform anime media ID

name: HandleGetContinuityWatchHistory
comments: HandleGetContinuityWatchHistory returns the continuity watch history.
@summary Returns the continuity watch history
@desc This endpoint is used to retrieve all watch history items.
@route /api/v1/continuity/history [GET]
@returns continuity.WatchHistory

api.summary: Returns the continuity watch history
descriptions: This endpoint is used to retrieve all watch history items.

name: HandleDirectorySelector
comments: HandleDirectorySelector returns directory content based on the input path.
@summary returns directory content based on the input path.
@desc This used by the directory selector component to get directory validation and suggestions.
@desc It returns subdirectories based on the input path.
@desc It returns 500 error if the directory does not exist (or cannot be accessed).
@route /api/v1/directory-selector [POST]
@returns handlers.DirectorySelectorResponse

api.summary: returns directory content based on the input path.
descriptions: This used by the directory selector component to get directory validation and suggestions.
It returns subdirectories based on the input path.
It returns 500 error if the directory does not exist (or cannot be accessed).

name: HandleOpenInExplorer

@desc It returns 'true' whether the operation was successful or not.
@route /api/v1/open-in-explorer [POST]
@returns bool

api.summary: opens the given directory in the file explorer.
descriptions: It returns 'true' whether the operation was successful or not.

descriptions: The total size of the cache files is returned in human-readable format.

name: HandleRemoveFileCacheBucket

@summary deletes all buckets with the given prefix.
@desc The bucket value is the prefix of the cache files that should be deleted.
@desc Returns 'true' if the operation was successful.
@route /api/v1/filecache/bucket [DELETE]

api.summary: deletes all buckets with the given prefix.
descriptions: The bucket value is the prefix of the cache files that should be deleted.
Returns 'true' if the operation was successful.

name: NewLibraryHandler
comments: NewLibraryHandler crea un handler de biblioteca con sus dependencias específicas.

name: HandleGetHomeCurated
comments: HandleGetHomeCurated returns the intelligent swimlanes for the home page.

name: HandleGetContinueWatching
comments: HandleGetContinueWatching returns the "Continue Watching" items for the user.

name: HandleRetagEpisodes
comments: HandleRetagEpisodes re-runs IntelligenceTagger on all LibraryEpisode records
using the titles and descriptions already stored in the DB — no TMDB API needed.
Trigger this via POST /api/v1/home/retag after adding new tag rules.

name: HandleGetLibraryExplorerFileTree
comments: HandleGetLibraryExplorerFileTree ...
@summary returns the file tree structure of the library directories.
@desc This returns a hierarchical representation of all directories and media files in the library.
@desc The tree includes LocalFile associations and media IDs for each file and directory.
@route /api/v1/library/explorer/file-tree [GET]
@returns library_explorer.FileTreeJSON

api.summary: returns the file tree structure of the library directories.
descriptions: This returns a hierarchical representation of all directories and media files in the library.
The tree includes LocalFile associations and media IDs for each file and directory.

name: HandleRefreshLibraryExplorerFileTree
comments: HandleRefreshLibraryExplorerFileTree ...
@summary refreshes the file tree structure of the library directories.
@desc This clears the cached file tree and rebuilds it from the current library state.
@desc Use this when the library structure has changed and you want to update the tree.
@route /api/v1/library/explorer/file-tree/refresh [POST]
@returns bool

api.summary: refreshes the file tree structure of the library directories.
descriptions: This clears the cached file tree and rebuilds it from the current library state.
Use this when the library structure has changed and you want to update the tree.

name: HandleLoadLibraryExplorerDirectoryChildren
comments: HandleLoadLibraryExplorerDirectoryChildren ...
@summary loads the children of a specific directory into the file tree.
@desc This endpoint loads directory children into the cached file tree. Frontend should re-fetch the tree afterwards.
@desc The directory path must be within the configured library paths for security.
@route /api/v1/library/explorer/directory-children [POST]
@returns bool

api.summary: loads the children of a specific directory into the file tree.
descriptions: This endpoint loads directory children into the cached file tree. Frontend should re-fetch the tree afterwards.
The directory path must be within the configured library paths for security.

name: HandleRequestMediastreamMediaContainer
comments: HandleRequestMediastreamMediaContainer requests a media stream container.
@summary request media stream.
@desc This requests a media stream and returns the media container to start the playback.
@returns mediastream.MediaContainer

This will also send an events.MediastreamShutdownStream event.
It will not return any error and is safe to call multiple times.

name: HandlePlatformListAnime
comments: HandlePlatformListAnime bridges legacy Platform list-anime calls to the active platform

name: HandlePlatformListRecentAiringAnime
comments: HandlePlatformListRecentAiringAnime bridges legacy Platform list-recent-anime calls to the active platform

name: HandleGetPlatformStats
comments: HandleGetPlatformStats returns viewer stats for the active platform

name: StartPlaybackHeartbeatSubscriber
comments: StartPlaybackHeartbeatSubscriber listens on the dispatcher for real-time
WebSocket playback progress events and updates the ContinuityManager.
This provides sub-second progress sync without HTTP request overhead.
Concurrency safety:
- Each heartbeat is processed sequentially within a single goroutine,
avoiding concurrent DB writes from multiple WS messages.
- The dispatcher's non-blocking publish ensures slow DB writes never
stall the WebSocket read loop or block other event subscribers.

name: HandlePlaybackSync
comments: HandlePlaybackSync ...
@summary receives playback telemetry from the frontend.
@desc    Updates continuity watch history and, when progress >= 85%,
automatically scrobbles the episode as watched to Platform.
@route /api/v1/playback/sync [POST]
@returns bool

api.summary: receives playback telemetry from the frontend.
descriptions: Updates continuity watch history and, when progress >= 85%,

name: HandleGetProgress
comments: HandleGetProgress handles GET /api/v1/progress

name: HandleSaveProgress
comments: HandleSaveProgress handles POST /api/v1/progress

name: rewriteURI
comments: rewriteURI rewrites a URI pointer if needed, returns true if modified

name: HandleResolveStreams
comments: HandleResolveStreams resolves all available playback sources for a given
episode using the UnifiedResolver.
@summary  Resolve all playback sources for an episode
@desc     Returns a list of ResolvedSource objects. Only local files
are supported in this architecture.
@returns  []core.ResolvedSource
@route    /api/v1/resolver/streams [GET]
Query parameters:
- mediaID  (required) — Platform media ID (positive) or TMDB ID (negative)
- episode  (required) — 1-based episode number
- kitsuId  (optional) — Kitsu anime ID; if absent, Torrentio is skipped

api.summary: Resolve all playback sources for an episode
descriptions: Returns a list of ResolvedSource objects. Only local files

name: JSONSuccess
comments: JSONSuccess serialises data as 200 OK inside APIResponse.Data.

name: JSONError
comments: JSONError serialises err as the given HTTP status inside APIResponse.Error.

name: NewDataResponse

name: NewErrorResponse
comments: NewErrorResponse wraps an error in an error envelope (legacy helper).

descriptions: This will update the app settings.
The client should re-fetch the server status after this.

name: HandleSaveSettings
comments: HandleSaveSettings updates the app settings.
@summary updates the app settings.
@desc Applies a PATCH-style merge: the incoming payload's non-nil sub-objects
@desc replace the stored ones; AutoDownloader is always merged from the DB to
@desc preserve scheduler state. Separate-table settings (Mediastream,
@desc Torrentstream, Debrid, Theme) are upserted only when present in payload.
@route /api/v1/settings [PATCH]
@returns handlers.Status

api.summary: updates the app settings.
descriptions: Applies a PATCH-style merge: the incoming payload's non-nil sub-objects
replace the stored ones; AutoDownloader is always merged from the DB to
preserve scheduler state. Separate-table settings (Mediastream,
Torrentstream, Debrid, Theme) are upserted only when present in payload.

name: NewStatus

It uses the RouteCtx to get the App instance containing the Database instance.

name: HandleGetStatus

descriptions: The server status includes app info, auth info and settings.
The client uses this to set the UI.
It is called on every page load to get the most up-to-date data.
It should be called right after updating the settings.

and caches it as a JPEG. Returns the cached image on subsequent requests.

name: TraceMiddleware
comments: TraceMiddleware injects a unique trace ID into every request context
and logs slow requests (>500ms) with their trace ID for correlation.

name: GetTraceID
comments: GetTraceID extracts the trace ID from the context. Returns empty string if not set.

name: generateTraceID
comments: generateTraceID creates a 16-character hex trace ID (8 random bytes).

@param episodeId - string - true - "The Episode ID or string seed"
@param duration - float - false - "The total duration of the video in seconds"

api.summary: returns video insights (heatmap)

params.name: episodeId
descriptions: The Episode ID or string seed

params.name: duration
descriptions: The total duration of the video in seconds

name: webSocketEventHandler
comments: webSocketEventHandler creates a new websocket handler for real-time event communication.
The route is registered BEFORE the auth middleware group so the HTTP→WS upgrade
is never blocked by a missing Authorization header (browsers can't send one during WS connect).
Clients that need auth send their token via ?token=<value> as a query parameter instead.
